leagueBriefing: West Ham United secured a spot in the FA Cup quarter-finals for the first time in a decade, overcoming Brentford in a dramatic penalty shootout at the London Stadium after a hard-fought 2-2 draw.

games.headline: Mavropanos converts winning penalty as West Ham edge Brentford in shootout thriller
games.brief: In a pulsating fifth-round encounter, West Ham United outlasted Brentford in a penalty shootout to set up a quarter-final clash with Leeds United. Jarrod Bowen twice put the Hammers ahead, first with a clinical close-range finish and then from the penalty spot, but each time the Bees responded through braces from Igor Thiago. After a scoreless extra-time period, the match was decided by Dango Ouattara's failed Panenka attempt, which Alphonse Areola comfortably saved. Konstantinos Mavropanos then stepped up to fire home the decisive spot-kick, sparking wild celebrations in East London as the Hammers remained perfect in the shootout.
bullets: Jarrod Bowen scored his seventh career goal against Brentford, the most he has scored against any single opponent.
Igor Thiago reached 20 goals for the season in all competitions with his second-half penalty equalizer.
Alphonse Areola was the hero of the shootout, standing his ground to catch Dango Ouattara's attempted chip down the middle.
The victory marks West Ham's first trip to the FA Cup quarter-finals since the 2015/16 season.
West Ham converted all five of their penalties (Bowen, Castellanos, Wilson, Souček, and Mavropanos) to win the shootout 5-3.
games.context: The win propels West Ham into a home quarter-final against Leeds United and keeps their hopes of silverware alive.

highlights.title: INSANE penalties send Hammers through! ⚒️ | West Ham (2-2) Brentford | Emirates FA Cup 2025-26
